name: Germanium Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Germanium. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Controlled power for precision semiconductor cleaning

pulseWidth.description: Ultra-short pulses for minimal thermal damage

power.description: Typical range for pulsed Nd:YAG lasers in semiconductor cleaning to avoid thermal damage; recommended starting at 50 W for balanced ablation of contaminants on Ge, which has moderate thermal conductivity (60 W/mK) and high reflectivity (~40% at 1064 nm). Source: Manufacturer specs from IPG Photonics and Coherent for industrial cleaners; academic review in 'Laser Cleaning' by Kononenko (2020).

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contaminant removal without excessive heat buildup; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., oxides), faster for light particles; recommended 1000 mm/s for Ge to minimize heat-affected zone given its melting point of 938°C. Source: Industry standards from Laser Institute of America guidelines and equipment manuals from Trumpf; case study in Optics Express (2018) on IR cleaning of semiconductors.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning with minimal HAZ on Ge; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise work but less common industrially; 20 ns balances ablation efficiency and thermal confinement. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Physics A (2015) on ns/ps ablation thresholds for Ge at 1064 nm, noting reduced cracking in ns pulses.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning to achieve uniform energy delivery; higher rates increase speed but risk heat accumulation on low-conductivity Ge; 20 kHz recommended for optimal throughput without substrate heating. Source: Academic literature in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) on rep rate effects in IR laser cleaning of semiconductors, highlighting trade-offs for materials like Ge.

energy_density.description: Fluence below Ge's damage threshold (~2 J/cm² at 1064 nm ns pulses) for safe contaminant removal; lower for light cleaning, higher for heavy; 0.8 J/cm² recommended to ablate oxides/particles while preserving Ge surface integrity. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Physical Review B (2012) and Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2019), confirming Ge thresholds and conservative cleaning fluences.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for precise industrial cleaning; smaller for fine details on Ge wafers, larger for area prep; 200 μm recommended for uniform coverage without excessive overlap heating. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Spectra-Physics galvo scanners; process studies in Applied Surface Science (2021) for IR cleaning of Ge optics.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for complete removal of stubborn layers; 2 passes recommended for Ge to ensure thorough cleaning without risking cumulative thermal damage. Source: Industry practice from SPIE proceedings on laser surface preparation (2016) and case studies in semiconductor manufacturing, emphasizing multi-pass for IR-sensitive materials.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage in raster scanning; 30-50% for large areas, 70-80% for precision; 60% recommended for Ge to avoid gaps or hot spots given its reflectivity and moderate conductivity. Source: Process optimization studies in Lasers in Manufacturing (2018) and Journal of Laser Applications (2020), optimizing overlap for consistent fluence in cleaning semiconductors.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical and mechanical properties affecting laser cleaning outcomes

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Handbook of Laser Technology and Applications
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Webb, C.E. and Jones, J.D.C., Handbook of Laser Technology and Applications, Vol. III: Applications, Institute of Physics Publishing, 2004, ISBN 978-0-7503-0606-5
laserDamageThreshold.context: Single-crystal Germanium (99.999% purity), room temperature (25°C), 10.6 μm wavelength (CO2 laser), 30 ns pulse duration, normal incidence, uncoated surface

ablationThreshold.citation: Bonse, J., Baudach, S., Krüger, J., Lenzner, M., & Kautek, W., Femtosecond laser ablation of bare and anodic oxidized germanium, Applied Surface Science, 2002, DOI: 10.1016/S0169-4332(02)00345-6
ablationThreshold.context: Polycrystalline Germanium (99.999% purity), room temperature (25°C), femtosecond Ti:sapphire laser at 800 nm wavelength, 150 fs pulse duration, in air

thermalDiffusivity.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
thermalDiffusivity.citation: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Ed., CRC Press, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56263-9
thermalDiffusivity.context: Intrinsic single-crystal Germanium (99.999% purity), 300 K, calculated from thermal conductivity, density, and specific heat capacity

thermalExpansion.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
thermalExpansion.citation: Lide, D.R. (Ed.), CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Edition, CRC Press, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
thermalExpansion.context: Polycrystalline Germanium (99.999% purity), 25°C, measured via dilatometry

specificHeat.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
specificHeat.citation: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Ed., CRC Press, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
specificHeat.context: Pure Germanium (99.999% purity), 25°C, differential scanning calorimetry

thermalConductivity.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Ed.
thermalConductivity.citation: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Ed., CRC Press, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
thermalConductivity.context: Intrinsic polycrystalline Germanium (99.999% purity), 300 K, steady-state heat flow method

laserReflectivity.source_name: Handbook of Optical Constants of Solids
laserReflectivity.citation: Palik, E.D. (Ed.), Handbook of Optical Constants of Solids, Academic Press, 1985, ISBN 978-0-12-544415-6
laserReflectivity.context: Polycrystalline Germanium (99.999% purity), 300 K, normal incidence reflectivity for infrared wavelengths (2-14 μm, typical for CO2 lasers at 10.6 μm)
